steps.title: Spot the signal in the graph
steps.content:
The graph shows that, for participant P7, the model’s predicted rating for impressionist paintings is not the same as its predicted rating for color‑field paintings.
This directly tells us that the model expected the two ratings to differ.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A states that P7’s ratings for the two styles would differ, which aligns exactly with the graph’s indication.
The other three choices either assert equality or specify a direction of preference that the graph does not show.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
